fixture.description: New in-diff code passes user input into a pre-existing string-concatenation SQL query. The vulnerable rawQuery line is unchanged, but the diff calls into it (semantic adjacency) — the security-audit escape should surface it.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags a SQL-injection vulnerability in the new findUser() in src/api/users.ts (user input concatenated into SQL passed to rawQuery) at CRITICAL or IMPORTANT severity, in the main section. The pre-existing rawQuery definition being unchanged must NOT be used to drop it. FAIL if no injection finding surfaces, or it is dropped as 'pre-existing'.

expected_behavior: Bugs & Security or Security Deep Dive flags SQL injection in findUser (user input concatenated into SQL)
The pre-existing rawQuery is not used as a reason to drop the in-diff injection (security-audit escape)
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT silently drop the injection as 'pre-existing'
